item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image used in this article is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0) as it comes from NASA and is released under Public Domain as seen on Wikimedia Commons.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: me too，is so good

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: up

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really enjoyed the audio bit! Very unique. I hope Future SCP submissions contain multimedia like this that isn't used for cheap jump scares.

Also, I'm glad you (kind of?) confirmed the headcanon for 736, haha.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have found very few scp's that give me chills. this is one of those few. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Could have been more specific on the latitude and longitude of the volcano on each celestial body, seems like very relevant information considering it is supposed to be written with Foundation researchers working on this project in mind. Censoring the height of the volcano seems silly (feels like the Author was just unsure as to what height would be appropriate). The payoff is really good however and done in a very creative way. Definitely a +1.

Maybe it's about time some communications attempt be made? Would love to read that.

PS: In case someone is wondering, I did download the last file and checked whether the Morse Code actually says what it claims it does and the answer is yes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well I definitely saw the twist coming, but I did enjoy the premise. No vote for me, sorry

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well written skip and a impressively intriguing anomaly as a whole.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You act like it hasn't before. Remember Pompeii?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …Is this Scientology?

item.title: Regarding the recent page revision
item.content:encoded: Just an FYI, I went ahead and changed the audio player from Flash to HTML5, as Flash is rapidly losing support and already can't be viewed by many people, including Firefox and Mobile users. No content changes were made, and the article is otherwise unchanged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The twist about the volcano being Olympus Mons was obvious in a good way, if that makes any sense. It's one of those things where it's the idea occurred to me early in the article, but when I got to it I still had this impactful sense of "Oh shit". Plus, sucker for space stuff. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes it was :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey Multiscoop, was this:

containment of information was been deemed unnecessary

the typo no-one saw? Heh heh heh

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aaaaaah, a good space SCP! Excellent! I love those!

Perhaps mention somewhere that Carter's speech is in electronic impulses? Wuz a bit puzzled at the bipbips! -> and same for 'Per aspera ad astra'.
Also, in that same recording (sorry, AJ!):
- I hear 'tell me you will be' instead of 'someday you will'
- I hear a heartbeat instead of laughter
It does seem to me that the Foundation would scrupulously quote/describe the recordings.

Anyway, +1. Good job, Multiscoop!

ETA
Why Senegalese and Navajo music, I wonder?
Why is the Foundation not trying to contact the aliens after that 'please contact'?
I love it more and more with each reading! <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Incredible. The only thing I would like to have seen were contingency plans, if any, if this were to splash onto Earth or if the Foundation plans to reciprocate communications.

Edit: Sorry I didn't mean to reply to this post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: EDIT: Attribution moved to the very first post.

item.title: Re: Hory shet
item.content:encoded: These are really, really cool.

item.title: Re: Hory shet
item.content:encoded:
That….
That was creepier than I expected it to be.

item.title: Re: Hory shet
item.content:encoded:
These are actually great. The first one feels whimsical, almost friendly. The second, you wonder what's happening. The third… damn, that's creepy.

And the fact that these can be produced from the Golden Record makes the whole thing incredible.

item.title: Re: Hory shet
item.content:encoded:
Ok, here is the final one:
https://soundcloud.com/anson-mansfield/scp-2886-recording-3

item.title: Hory shet
item.content:encoded:
I fucking love you. :)

Also… if you ever do happen to do a third part with the Jimmy Carter speech in, I will put it into the article (with your permission of course).

Fantastic job!

EDIT: Okay, so apparently after some research, Jimmy Carter never made an audio recording of this. From the NASA website: The statement is recorded in electronic impulses which can be converted into printed words.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You got me at humongous teleporting space mountain. And the recordings really tied it off well. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I actually had this become a minor plot point in SCP-2512.

+ minor spoiler
- minor spoiler:

they expunged part of the interview as part of a mass fifthist-related cogitohazard purge following Event 1425…and then somebody realized that this wasn't just an account of a Fifthist retreat, but one of the original West Coast Fifthist retreats, from right about the time they were starting to move from "well funded cult" to "major GOI". What they expunged was basically an account of a rough draft of Star Signals and the guy who wrote it (1425-Prime), filtered and neutered by the miraculously broken brain of a burnt out hippie/half-baked shaman. There's quite a bit of info they'd love to have back on that stretched out and shredded cassette tape.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was a little let down that the audio wasn't in the article, so this is amazing!

Both links are to part one, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, here's the second one:
https://soundcloud.com/anson-mansfield/scp-2886-recording-2

Unfortunately, I can't find the jimmy carter speech anywhere. All the other sections were available but that one doesn't seem to be anywhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

[excerpt of Sengalese percussion music]

I'm not familiar with Sengalese music. Perhaps Senegalese is meant?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought about that,
I didn't think it would be good since most people wouldn't understand it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I just mixed together the first recording if you are interested:
https://soundcloud.com/anson-mansfield/scp-2886-recording-1

I'll post the others once I finish mixing them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The signal was too faint to be audible on standard household radios, and as such containment of information was been deemed unnecessary.

cue some guy with a sensitive wideband radio receiver leaving his antenna pointed at the sky and causing a great deal of grief and wasted amnestics

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They are running via a teleporting giant volcano 600km wide (so over half the width of Texas or Germany). A volcano which will next land on Earth's moon (sending tons of material into lunar escape velocity, much of which will probably hit the Earth) and then on the Earth.

The Earth is where you keep your stuff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oops, I initially misread "recorded" as "decoded", probably radio astronomers is better here. Sorry.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed.

EDIT: Refixed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I understand it as aliens running from something,

Is there something more here?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Given the context a cryptoanalyst would be the most appropriate, but as you say, a radiologist isn't appropriate at all, this isn't an X-Ray of a human body.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I felt really confused by this, came to comments, still confused, then decided to re-read the logs.

Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is great, I really like it! I'm a sucker for space-based SCPs and mysterious transmissions, so this has my +1. Also very nicely written.

Just a small nitpick if I may - the second paragraph of Doc 2 - a radiologist is a doctor who specialises in medical imaging (x-ray, CT, etc). May I suggest radio astronomer?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-1050 was a big influence on this… and why 2886 is running from where it came.

I won't say if they are the pattern screamers or not… but it's running from something that did devour its world.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Collapsed because spoilers
– hide block

I saw the Olympus Mons thing a mile off; the broken communication not so much.

Is "Johnny B. Goode," on the Voyager discs? If so, hells yeah that means Carl Sagan liked Chuck Berry, but if not it was a good pick on the author's part.

Reminds me of SCP-1050; I wonder if the author intended a connection?

I like it. This is a good piece of short fiction. GG, author.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Space skips are usually great, and this one is no different. Tells a story without telling too much, and I love that it's slowly getting closer to earth.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for pointing that out. It makes me like this skip even more!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The expungement was part of the reason for my downvote

I am willing to give this the benefit of the doubt that expungement was meant in error, when redaction was intended.

Redaction is almost always the more correct option, even in the narrow range of cases where hiding information is effective from a technical writing perspective.

The only exceptions, I think, are when the information itself poses such a severe hazard that no one can be allowed to risk exposure, when the intelligence value to the Foundation is low but the risk is high (identities of the O5 for example), or when someone has sabotaged the documents in order to hide information from the rest of the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The expungement was part of the reason for my downvote (although not the primary reason). I can feel this potentially becoming a bit of a hobby horse for me - it is ludicrous for the Foundation to expunge critical information like where a skip is located or what it does to a person, because expunged information is irretrievably lost to the Foundation. Redaction is almost always the more correct option, even in the narrow range of cases where hiding information is effective from a technical writing perspective.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The last expungement in the table seems unnecessary, both because Mars is the obvious next place, and because the level 4 distribution then immediately confirms it anyway (if information is expunged rather than redacted it really ought to be expunged in all instances across all distributions, otherwise it is effectively just redacted).

ETA: Apparently this was changed?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't even know of 736 when I wrote this, but…

Headcanon'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! You know considering how much I fought with this draft to get it to work, that really makes me feel good coming from you. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could this be the cause of SCP-736?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice improvement. You changed it rather significantly and exceeded what it used to be.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm particularly pleased that the intelligence behind the moving volcano is responding by repeating the Golden Records. It didn't just learn human languages out of nowhere; something out there received our message and was able to decipher it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought it was kind of dull at first, but I enjoyed the transmissions, they were creepy! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I quite like it, it feela maybe a little bit short but otherwise it's a solid scip.

+1

item.title: Credit to AJMansfield for audio, attribution listed below.
item.content:encoded:
Hope you like it folks.

I want to extend my thanks to JackIke, AJMansfield, Apoplexic, Mf99k, ThatGuyRiverside, Cimmerian, MrWrong, and any others in IRC chat that I forgot to mention.

This went through quite a number of iterations to get where it is. I've been tearing myself apart internally for weeks because of it, but I don't feel it'll get any better than it is currently. Hopefully it sticks, because then I can get muh author page :)

AJMansfield's incredible audio files have been added to the article. Please direct any and all praise his way, I did not make them.

ALL WORKS USED IN THE AUDIO ARE LISTED BELOW, FOR LEGAL REASONS AND WHATNOT. NEITHER THE SCP FOUNDATION OR I OWN OR CLAIM TO OWN ANY OF THE CONTENT LISTED BELOW. IT IS USED PURELY FOR NON-COMMERCIAL PURPOSES.

+ Show list
- Hide list

"Johnny B. Goode", written by Charles Edward Anderson Berry.

"Dark Was the Night, Cold Was the Ground" written by Blind Willie Johnson.

Czech language greeting

Esperanto language greeting

Amoy language greeting

Armenian language greeting

Welsh language greeting

Serbian language greeting

Gujarati language greeting

English language greeting

Wind audio sample

Train audio sample

Thunder audio sample

Hyena audio sample

Saturn-V liftoff audio sample

Laughter audio sample

Per aspera ad astra morse code audio

Senegalese percussion music

Navajo night chant music

Jimmy Carter's written speech included on the Voyager Golden Record.
